titleStats.name: a zero-fill solver gets a full row of zeros with null firstFill; a single-entry trace has no stall
titleStats.intent: TITLES.md pool ruling: a solver appearing in the events at all (u2's one wrong write) owns a row; fills 0, firstFill/lastFill null, every count zero, focus pinned 0 (not NaN). With T = 1 the single trace entry is both the opening and the closing stretch (ceil(OPENING_SHARE * 1) = 1), span is 0 for a single fill, moments().turningPoint is null so nobody brokeStall and room.stallSeconds is 0.

titleStats.name: a wrong write to an empty cell is a wrongWrite, never an overwrite; destroying another's matching cell is an overwrite
titleStats.intent: TITLES.md overwrite boundary, replayed in seq order with a running board: u2's Z onto empty c1 is wrongWrite only; u2's Z onto matching c0 (trace owner u1) is BOTH a wrongWrite and an overwrite; u2's clear of the then-non-matching c0 is neither (the board did not match); u2's later clear of the re-corrected c0 is an overwrite but not a wrongWrite (a clear writes no value).

titleStats.name: rebus: a first-char-correct write is not a wrongWrite; upgrading a correct first char to the full rebus is not an overwrite
titleStats.intent: Correctness is everywhere the engine comparator `matches`, never string equality (TITLES.md, FIRST-CORRECT.md): u1's "S" against solution "STAR" matches (D12 first-char acceptance), so it is a fill, not a wrongWrite. u1's "STAR" over own "S" and u1's "TEN" over u2's matching "T" are correct-to-correct rewrites: the replacement still matches, so neither is an overwrite, whoever owns the cell.

titleStats.name: clobbering your OWN first-correct cell is never an overwrite (owner ruling: second-guessing, not sabotage)
titleStats.intent: TITLES.md overwrite definition requires the trace owner to be another solver: u1's Z over own matching c0 counts as a wrongWrite but not an overwrite; u1's clear of own matching c1 counts as neither. u2 doing the same to u1's c2 is the contrast: overwrite and wrongWrite both.

titleStats.name: slotsStarted, slotsFinished, meddles, and slotsTouched over a full 2x3
titleStats.intent: TITLES.md slot stats: a slot's first/last trace entry among its cells by (at, seq) names its starter/finisher; meddles are slots finished by u but started by someone else; slotsTouched counts distinct slots holding at least one of u's fills (one cell touches both its across and its down slot).

titleStats.name: opening and closing stretches at an exact-integer share: T = 10 takes exactly 2 entries, the 3rd-earliest is outside
titleStats.intent: TITLES.md: openingFills/closingFills are the owned entries among the first/last ceil(OPENING_SHARE * T) of the (at, seq)-ordered trace, OPENING_SHARE = 0.2. With T = 10, ceil(2) = 2 exactly: u1 owns the first three entries but only two are opening; u2 owns the last two, its closing pair. Ordinal, not temporal.

titleStats.name: opening/closing at T=15, the exact-integer boundary: window is ceil(T/5)=3, so the 4th-earliest and 4th-latest entries are OUTSIDE the stretch
titleStats.intent: TITLES.md: openingFills/closingFills slice the first/last ceil(OPENING_SHARE * T) of the (at, seq)-ordered trace, OPENING_SHARE = 0.2 = 1/5. At T = 15 the window is exactly ceil(15/5) = 3; the share is a rational fifth, never a float multiply that could round 0.2*15 up to a 4th entry. u2 owns only the 4th-earliest and 4th-latest entries (1-based), both just outside the size-3 opening and closing stretches, so its openingFills and closingFills are 0; u1 owns the three opening and three closing entries. Ordinal, not temporal (INV-9).

titleStats.name: clock skew: at-order and seq-order disagree; opening/firstFill follow (at, seq), brokeStall follows the shipped moments() seq-order gap scan
titleStats.intent: THE SKEW CASE. The trace in seq order is c0(at 100000, u2), c1(at 5000, u1), c2(at 110000, u1). openingFills and closingFills order the trace by (at, seq): with T = 3, ceil(0.2 * 3) = 1, so the opening entry is c1 (u1, the earliest at despite seq 2) and the closing entry is c2 (u1). firstFill is the earliest owned entry by (at, seq): u1 gets {5000, 2}. brokeStall is pinned byte-identical to the shipped moments() (packages/engine/src/analysis.ts), whose gap scan walks CONSECUTIVE trace entries in seq order: gaps are -95000 then +105000, so the break is c2 (u1) and stallSeconds is 105. An (at, seq)-ordered scan would instead find a 95s stall broken by c0 (u2); where the two orderings disagree, the moments() behavior wins, so the ribbon's marker and the ice-breaker card can never name different people.

titleStats.name: a fill at exactly t + BURST_WINDOW_MS is inside the closed window; one millisecond past is outside
titleStats.intent: TITLES.md: burst is the max count of a solver's own fills inside any closed window [t, t + BURST_WINDOW_MS], BURST_WINDOW_MS = 30000, the same inclusivity as momentum's burst. u1's fills at 0, 10000, 30000 all sit in [0, 30000] (the endpoint is IN), burst 3; u2's fills at 30001 and 60002 never share a window (60002 > 30001 + 30000 by one ms), burst 1. The room's largest trace gap is 30001 ms, floored to 30 whole seconds.

titleStats.name: quadrants on a 5x5: the split is at ceil(5/2) = 3, the center cell is top-left, row/col 3 land bottom/right
titleStats.intent: TITLES.md quadrant rule, 0-indexed: a cell is top when row < ceil(rows/2) and left when col < ceil(cols/2). On a 5x5 the split index is 3, so rows 0-2 are top and the center cell 12 (r2, c2) is TOP-LEFT, while cell 17 (r3, c2) is bottom-left, cell 13 (r2, c3) top-right, cell 18 (r3, c3) bottom-right. u1's busiest quadrant is top-left with 3 of 6 fills (focus 0.5); u2's is bottom-right with 2 of 4 (focus 0.5). spread is distinct rows plus distinct cols over owned entries.

titleStats.name: span and stallSeconds are whole seconds, floored: a 90750 ms gap reports 90
titleStats.intent: TITLES.md: all whole seconds are floor(). u1's two fills sit 90750 ms apart: span floor(90.75) = 90, and the same gap is the trace's largest, so room.stallSeconds is 90, never 91 (no rounding). u1 owns the break entry, brokeStall 1.

titleStats.name: starred slots ARE the marquee set, even beside an equally long unstarred slot; a tied slot has no leader
titleStats.intent: TITLES.md marquee signal 1: when any slot is starred the marquee set is exactly the starred slots, so the unstarred length-8 A-slot [0..7] is out despite passing MARQUEE_MIN_LENGTH. u1 owns 5 of the starred [8..15]'s first-correct cells against u2's 2 and u3's 1: one lead. The starred [0, 8] splits 1-1 between u2 and u1: strictly-more fails, a tied slot has no leader, so it counts for nobody.

titleStats.name: no stars: the marquee is every slot within the top two distinct lengths (9 and 7), the short filler is out
titleStats.intent: TITLES.md marquee signal 2 (the fallback): with no starred slot, the marquee set is every slot whose length is within the top two distinct lengths, gated to length >= MARQUEE_MIN_LENGTH = 7. Lengths here are {9, 7, 2}: both 9s and the 7 are marquee, the length-2 filler is not, so u2's lead on the filler counts for nothing. u1 leads both length-9 slots (2 first-correct cells to 1); u2 leads the length-7 slot.

titleStats.name: the length tier is gated at MARQUEE_MIN_LENGTH: a length-6 slot in the top two distinct lengths is still out
titleStats.intent: TITLES.md marquee signal 2: the top two distinct lengths here are {8, 6}, but the gate is length >= MARQUEE_MIN_LENGTH = 7, so only the length-8 slot is marquee. u2 owns 3 of 6 first-correct cells of the length-6 slot and leads it, worth nothing; u1 leads the length-8 slot 2 to 1.

titleStats.name: THE REFERENCE SOLVE: a full 5x5 (blocks at 4 and 20), three solvers, every column of the sheet asserted
titleStats.intent: The debugging fixture: a complete room producing the full stat sheet. "ub" opens with a four-fill streak (openingFills 4 of the ceil(0.2 * 23) = 5-entry opening stretch, burst 4) and returns for the last two squares. "uh" grinds 12 accurate fills and breaks the room's 90-second stall (brokeStall 1, but 90 < STALL_FLOOR_SECONDS). "us" arrives late, guesses wrong at c17 then corrects it, and destroys three of the others' correct squares (overwrites 3: two wrong writes and one clear; wrongWrites 3: c17's guess plus the two overwriting Zs). Repairs by uh and ub change no trace entry (scheme 1). Every slot length is at most 5, below MARQUEE_MIN_LENGTH = 7 and nothing is starred, so a mini has no marquee tier and every marqueeLeads is 0. The awardTitles cluster walks this exact sheet in its reference case.

awardTitles.name: solo rule: one event-member solver titles nobody, whatever the sheet says
awardTitles.intent: TITLES.md solo rule: a completed game with fewer than two event-member solvers has titles []. u1's row passes the bullseye, sprinter, and ice-breaker gates outright, and still nothing awards: a superlative is social.

awardTitles.name: two event members where only one has fills: titles award (the pool is event membership, not trace membership)
awardTitles.intent: TITLES.md pool + solo rulings: the racer u2 typed (one wrong write, zero fills) so the room has two event members and titles are NOT empty. u1 takes bullseye (wrongWrites 0, fills >= BULLSEYE_MIN_FILLS = 5). u2 passes no gate (every other gate consumes fills, trace entries, or slots) and gets nothing: the floor guarantee covers solvers with fills >= 1 only. The ice-breaker gate also refuses here: u1 broke the stall but 90 < STALL_FLOOR_SECONDS = 120.

awardTitles.name: the zero-fill saboteur: 0 fills, 3 overwrites of others' correct squares, titled at rung 1
awardTitles.intent: TITLES.md pool ruling, stated exactly: everyone who typed at all is in the pool, and a zero-fill solver can only ever be saboteur. u2 passes the saboteur gate (overwrites >= SABOTEUR_MIN = 3) with no fills at all and takes rung 1 with its evidence; u1 takes bullseye behind them.

awardTitles.name: the specialty showcase: one-hit-wonder (latest firstFill), ice-breaker, headliner, meddler, quick-starter, and closer each award
awardTitles.intent: Every specialty rung the other cases leave dark awards here, in ladder order (TITLES.md v1 ladder). s1 and s7 both pass the one-hit-wonder gate (fills == 1, wrongWrites == 0, room max fills 6 >= 3); the claim is the LATEST firstFill by (at, seq), so s1 (at 200000) beats s7 (at 100000), and the cameo's evidence is none (null). s2 owns the break of a 150s stall (>= STALL_FLOOR_SECONDS = 120): ice-breaker, evidence the room's stallSeconds. Bullseye refuses everyone: the clean rows (s2, s7) sit under BULLSEYE_MIN_FILLS = 5 and the big fillers all have a wrongWrite. s3 leads 2 marquee slots: headliner. Sprinter refuses (max burst 3 < SPRINTER_MIN_BURST = 4). s4 meddles 3 (>= MEDDLER_MIN = 2): meddler. s5 owns 3 opening-stretch fills: quick-starter; s6 owns 2 closing: closer. s7, the losing cameo, still gets its guaranteed floor title: specialist.

awardTitles.name: THE COVERAGE THEOREM: every specialty gate fails by a hair, and all six fillers still title off the floor
awardTitles.intent: TITLES.md floor tier: a floor rung can never refuse while an untitled solver with a fill remains, so six fillers in a specialty-dark room all title. Each specialty gate is refused at its boundary: saboteur at 2 overwrites (SABOTEUR_MIN = 3, f1); one-hit-wonder because the cameo f2 has a wrongWrite (room max fills 8 >= 3, so the refusal is on clean hands); ice-breaker at stallSeconds 119 (STALL_FLOOR_SECONDS = 120, f3 owns the break); bullseye because nobody is clean; headliner on an empty marquee (all marqueeLeads 0); sprinter at burst 3 (SPRINTER_MIN_BURST = 4, f4); meddler at 1 meddle (MEDDLER_MIN = 2, f6); quick-starter and closer at zero opening/closing fills. The floor then walks: specialist resolves the f1/f2 focus tie (1 vs 1) by MORE FILLS to f1; long-hauler's room-wide argmax f1 (span 500) is already titled, so the claim runs over the untitled and f3's 400 wins; wanderer f4 (evidence none); scribbler f5; collector f6; and workhorse catches the last filler, f2, evidence 1.

awardTitles.name: the coverage theorem's edge: a SEVENTH filler leaves exactly one solver untitled (the documented remainder)
awardTitles.intent: TITLES.md floor tier: coverage is arithmetic, six floor rungs deep. The same room plus a seventh filler f7 (mediocre everywhere, so it wins no earlier rung) still awards all six floor titles, but workhorse's max-fills claim among the last two untitled now prefers f7 (4 fills) over the cameo f2 (1 fill): f2 ends untitled and renders nothing, never a placeholder. Deepening the floor for bigger rooms is a vector diff, not a design change.

awardTitles.name: already-titled fall-through: the sprinter argmax is the saboteur, so the rung falls to the next eligible; a single-claimant rung lapses
awardTitles.intent: TITLES.md ladder walk: each solver receives at most one title, and a rung whose argmax winner is already titled falls to the next eligible solver. u1 takes saboteur (overwrites 4 >= SABOTEUR_MIN), then u1's burst 6 tops the sprinter column, so the rung falls to u2 (burst 4, evidence 4: the WINNER'S number rides the card, never the titled argmax's). The ice-breaker rung shows the lapse: u1 owns the break of a 200s stall (gate passes) but is titled, and no other solver owns a break, so the rung awards nobody. u3 is caught by the floor: specialist.

awardTitles.name: "tie by fills": an equal focus resolves to MORE fills, not to the universal earlier-firstFill rule
awardTitles.intent: TITLES.md determinism: where a rung says "tie by fills", more fills wins first, then the universal rule. u1 and u2 tie on focus 0.8; u2 has 10 fills to u1's 5 and takes specialist, though the universal rule alone would prefer u1's earlier firstFill. Every specialty gate is quiet (both dirty, bursts under SPRINTER_MIN_BURST, one meddle under MEDDLER_MIN, no marquee, no opening/closing fills, stall under STALL_FLOOR_SECONDS); u1 falls to long-hauler.

awardTitles.name: the universal tie-break, both layers: earlier at wins first, then lower seq; and it engages only after "tie by fills" exhausts
awardTitles.intent: TITLES.md determinism: every tie breaks by the earlier firstFill, ordered by (at, seq). Three solvers tie the saboteur column at 3 overwrites: u3's firstFill at 4000 beats both at-5000 rows, seq notwithstanding (at is primary). At the specialist rung, u1 and u2 then tie on focus AND on fills, so the universal rule runs: both firstFills sit at 5000 and u2's seq 4 beats u1's seq 9 (seq is the secondary key).

awardTitles.name: the universal tie-break: a solver with a fill sorts before every zero-fill solver, however late the fill
awardTitles.intent: TITLES.md determinism: a solver with no fills sorts after every solver with one. Three solvers tie saboteur at 3 overwrites; uz's single (very late, at 900000) fill still ranks it ahead of the zero-fill ua and ub, so uz is the saboteur. uz's clears-only rampage keeps wrongWrites 0 (a clear writes no value), and the one-hit-wonder rung then finds no untitled gate-passer: uz is titled and the zero-fill pair has no fill. Nobody else has a fill, so the floor is empty and the walk ends with one title.

awardTitles.name: the universal tie-break's last resort: two identical zero-fill saboteurs resolve by ascending ASCII userId
awardTitles.intent: TITLES.md determinism (INV-1): the final tie is ascending ASCII userId. ua and ub both have 3 overwrites and no fills (firstFill null on both), so every earlier key exhausts and "ua" < "ub" by ASCII: ua is the saboteur. The filler um takes the first floor rung, specialist.

awardTitles.name: THE REFERENCE WALK: the reference solve's exact sheet walks the full ladder to saboteur, bullseye, sprinter
awardTitles.intent: The companion to the titleStats reference case: its output, verbatim (lastFill and the slot start/finish counts included; awardTitles ignores what the ladder does not read). The walk, rung by rung: (1) saboteur takes us (overwrites 3 >= SABOTEUR_MIN). (2) one-hit-wonder: nobody has fills == 1. (3) ice-breaker: uh owns the break but the 90s stall is under STALL_FLOOR_SECONDS = 120: refused. (4) bullseye: the clean rows are ub (6 fills) and uh (12); max fills takes uh, evidence 12. (5) headliner: a mini has no marquee, all marqueeLeads 0: refused. (6) sprinter: ub's opening streak burst 4 meets SPRINTER_MIN_BURST exactly (uh sits at 3): ub, evidence 4. Rungs 7-15 find every solver titled and award nobody. Output is ordered by ladder rank, one title per solver.
